questions.question: Prove that every non-empty set of positive integers has a least element using the Well Ordering Principle. Consider the following statement: 'For all positive integers n, if S is a non-empty set of positive integers, then S has a least element.' What is the base case and the inductive step of this mathematical induction?

Consider the statement: 'For all positive integers n, if k is a subset of the set {1, 2, 3, ..., n} then k has a least element.' State the base case and the inductive step. Then, using mathematical induction, prove the statement.
A set of positive integers S is said to be 'well ordered' if every non-empty subset of S has a least element. Prove that the set of all positive integers is well ordered using mathematical induction.
